description:

=== THE THESIS (LOCKED — Fable fresh-eyes synthesis, adopted by Zig 2026-07-15; full detail .local/research/mcp-fable-fresh-eyes.md) ===
Title (working): "Earned Autonomy: Governing Agent Fleets on the 2026-07-28 MCP Spec."
The bridge: the 2026-07-28 spec turns agent activity into structured, attributable, enforceable events; a higher-level delivery-outcome/context layer turns those events into answers and steering; together they close the loop that lets an org run autonomous work on evidence. CELEBRATE MCP as the enabler (the governable substrate), never fault it.

THE LAYER (the de-fuzzed part) = ONE STORE, TWO FACES. Write path ATTRIBUTES delivery events to agent-authored change; read path SERVES the distillation back to agents as governed typed context AND to leaders as ROI. Four capabilities: ATTRIBUTE (authorship-segment delivered change; join MCP action records to VCS/CI-CD) . EVALUATE (outcome signals per agent change: change-fail/revert/rework/override/held-in-prod + cost-to-outcome = Ben's ROI verbatim) . SERVE (deterministic typed freshness-contracted context back to agents over the 7-28 substrate: 2106 typed / 2549 cache / 2567 per-call authz / 414 traces) . STEER (evaluations -> boundary policy). STEER is IN, framed as FORWARD AGENDA with boundary conditions first-class.

THE SPINE (novel, open lane) = EARNED AUTONOMY. MCP governs itself by measurement (SEP-2484 conformance-gates Standards-Track SEPs; SDK tiers w/ public relegation after 4 wks; 12-month lifecycle clock SEP-2596/2577 -- grounded refs/projects/mcp.md:344-379). Port to agent fleets: autonomy earned by delivered outcomes, adjusted at the per-call enforcement point the spec provides. MCP = actuator + legibility; the layer = sensor + evidence; the policy engine between = the org's build (flag honestly). Boundary conditions: attribution lag (rolling windows) . unstable identity (unit of trust = a WORKFLOW CONFIG, not an agent instance) . Goodhart (gate on held-in-prod, never activity counts) . small-n (sandbox defaults).

KEY ACCURACY FIX: do NOT anchor the two-plane gap to statelessness/SEP-2567 as if removing sessions created it (a maintainer punctures it: sessions were correlation state, never a value ledger). The gap was ALWAYS there; 2567 proves the ENABLING half (per-call authz + identity). Survivable claim: the protocol's horizon ends at the tool boundary; no MCP call runs at merge/deploy/incident time.
TWO HONESTY VALVES (or a CISO/maintainer punctures it): (1) per-call authz = an ENFORCEMENT POINT; scope semantics stay the implementer's design (authN != authZ-scope). (2) per-call identity = authenticated principal + client (clientInfo/OAuth/EMA-ID-JAG), NOT individual-agent attribution -- agent-level attribution is a convention you build (baggage/metadata), never a spec grant. Both valves STRENGTHEN the thesis.
CUT the defensive 'design property not immaturity' section. Keep one sentence: the spec's delegate-observability posture (Logging->stderr/OTel, trace to your backend) shows where measurement lives -- in your systems, over standard rails.
6-SECTION SPINE (prose): (1) the question that predates the spec (ROI on autonomous work; NANDA 95% + DORA-2025 amplifier as stakes) (2) what 7-28 makes legible + enforceable (the SEP walk; celebration earned) (3) the loop above the protocol (atoms don't compose into answers; horizon ends at the tool boundary) (4) the delivery context layer defined (4 capabilities) (5) earned autonomy (conformance-culture port + boundary conditions + 2 valves) (6) recommendations + evaluation checklist. Menu for Alex: (e) governance spine + (c) enterprise scale.

ADOPT Fable's synthesis as the whitepaper spine -- 'Earned Autonomy': the 2026-07-28 spec makes agent activity structured/attributable/enforceable; a delivery context layer (one store, two faces: ATTRIBUTE/EVALUATE write path + SERVE read path + STEER) turns events into answers + steering; together they close the loop. Port MCP's own govern-by-measurement (conformance gates, SDK tiers, relegation) to agent fleets. Statelessness anchor RELOCATED (don't fault SEP-2567; celebrate it as the enabling half). Two honesty valves added; the defensive 'design property' section cut.
2. STEER is IN, framed as FORWARD AGENDA with boundary conditions (attribution lag, Goodhart, unstable identity=trust-attaches-to-workflow-config, small-n) first-class. Fallback (drop Steer) held in reserve, not taken.
3. BLOG (aaif-uf3) is the ACCESSIBLE LEAD of the same idea -- one idea at two altitudes. Entry hook 'the spec that measures itself' -> the top level of earned autonomy, told simply -> directs into the whitepaper. Publishes Jul 29 for the Jul 31 DI segment (post-Final; dodges the AMD week).

METHODOLOGY: run the whitepaper as a RESEARCH PAPER under the ~/cfp/mise production loop wholesale (research -> infra -> per-section worktree writing subagents -> assembly -> REVIEW-NOTES human gate -> gated publish -> post-publish revision sub-arc; every claim a grep-able test case; critic loop before every merge). Why: executes Zig's polarity reframe rigorously (celebrate MCP, don't fault it); de-fuzzes the higher-level layer into a concrete 4-capability store that matches Ben's + Zig's own verbatim language (Ben line 281 'right data to make decisions + automation that enacts'; Zig line 252 'a tool the factory uses to measure and understand itself'); finds a novel framework spine (govern-by-measurement ported to fleets) in the open governance lane nobody owns; fixes a real accuracy hole a maintainer could have punctured. Steer answers Ben's 'automation that enacts on the data' and is the boldest/most-novel move; forward-agenda framing keeps it credible.

Reversibility: fully reversible pre-draft -- thesis lives only in .local + beads; nothing published or submitted (hard gate intact). Steer can drop to the Attribute/Evaluate/Serve fallback at /spec or draft time at low cost. Blog angle/timing reversible until DI material is due. title: setup: /aaif-radar skill — weekly AAIF submissions-landscape scanner (trends, over-rotation, opportunities, grading calibration)
description:
A project-local skill (.claude/skills/aaif-radar/) that scans the aaif/ambassadors submissions repo weekly (read-only, via gh api) and answers: what's being submitted + scored, where's the bloat/over-rotation, what lanes are under-served (our opportunities/inspiration), and is Angie's grading still matching our documented model.

TWO OUTPUTS (hard-rule split — CRITICAL):
1. FULL report -> .local/radar/YYYY-Www.md (PRIVATE, gitignored): may reference specific participants/submissions (landscape awareness). Includes a running ledger so week-over-week trends work (delta since last run).
2. DISTILLED note -> a CLOSED 'note' bead in aaif's .beads (COMMITTED): participant-FREE. Only the generalized model — type/project distribution trends, scoring calibration (new types seen, rejections, drift vs our TYPE->BASE_POINTS map), and opportunity lanes framed around developer value. NO named peers, NO ranking, NO 'better than X'. This preserves what we learn without violating the no-gossip rule.

ALSO: if notable (over-rotation, a fresh opportunity, a scoring-model drift), file a P1 human: bead + push notification. Keep /aaif-review's model calibrated: flag any observed type->points that diverges from aaif-review-system.md.

MODES: interactive (Zig types /aaif-radar) may surface via AskUserQuestion; autonomous (timer-fired) NEVER blocks — writes report + note bead, notifies if notable, ends. Read-only: never posts to any AAIF surface.

Grounding: .local/research/aaif-review-system.md (the scoring model + schema), CLAUDE.md hard rules, the existing /aaif-review skill (participant-free model). Pairs with R2 (the systemd timer).

GOAL: spec a lean, DELIBERATE 'nucleus' of real tools (the baseline every practitioner wants: read, write, fetch + a few essentials), federated + curated behind agentgateway (virtual MCP + CEL mcpAuthorization), replacing the shim. This becomes the tutorial's centerpiece + screenshots ('scope your own lean goose toolkit').

Dispatched a research/design agent to produce the spec: name candidates (on-theme), exact tool selection w/ rationale, VERIFIED agentgateway 1.3.1 config (virtual-MCP targets + CEL curation), goose wiring, local-model serving, and a verification plan. Then /check -> /test -> /impl -> wire up. NAME is Zig's final call (see AskUserQuestion). Two-tier: keep infra generic in any public-bound material.
notes: SPEC COMPLETE (agent, verified vs agentgateway 1.3.1 source + goose 1.41 + live probes). NAME = 'honk' (Zig 2026-07-04). Nucleus = 6 tools / 2 servers: filesystem read_text_file/write_file/edit_file/list_directory/search_files + fetch. Showcase exclusion = move_file (invisible+uncallable+logged, the V3 negative test). Verified agentgateway config (virtual MCP targets + CEL mcpAuthorization; gotcha: mcp.tool.name is UN-prefixed). goose wiring = streamable_http extension 'honk' replacing the disabled shim. Local model = Qwen3-8B-GGUF Q4_K_M via goose local-models OR ollama qwen3:8b (context trap: raise OLLAMA_CONTEXT_LENGTH to 32k). V1-V4 verification plan + 4 honest verify-during-build unknowns. title: setup: tutorial art direction — retrospective + /randomize composition plan + new-aesthetics research (images TABLED 2026-07-04)
description:
IMAGES TABLED (Zig, 2026-07-04) to focus on honk + screenshots + tutorial. This bead holds the art-direction retrospective + the plan for when we return.

WHAT WE TRIED (submissions/2026-07-harness-loop-gateway/images/):
- Round 1 (8 styles of a goose piloting a biplane): retrofuturist-pop, aviation-deco, blueprint, flat-vector, risograph, watercolor, pop-comic, isometric-3D. Zig liked #1 (retrofuturist), #4 (flat-vector), #6 (watercolor), #8 (isometric).
- Round 2: refined those 4 + 4 blends. Zig initially liked the painterly diorama (b6x8) but ultimately rejected the diorama direction.
- Header pass (AAIF-recolor of the diorama): rejected -- colors bolder but not matching.
- Pivot: multi-ref (batch1 #1 + the AAIF ambassador logo), recolor to black+orange, pair the ambassador CHARACTER with the goose as a 'Where in the World is Carmen Sandiego' vigilante duo, strict NO-TEXT control. char = too extreme / black-on-black / illegible. char2 = legible full-palette (black+orange dominant + periwinkle/cream), better but still rejected.

WHAT WE LEARNED / DIDN'T LIKE (constraints for next time):
1. Black+orange must be DOMINANT but NOT the only colors -- need a full, legible palette (periwinkle, cream, sky tones, real value range). Pure black-on-black = illegible.
2. MASCOT FIDELITY (critical): the AAIF ambassador is an ALL-BLACK, FACELESS silhouette. Its ONLY facial feature is the round ORANGE monocle-lens; it also has a black trench coat, a black fedora with an orange hatband, and a small antenna with an orange tip. The generations wrongly gave it a face/eyes -- MUST prompt: faceless solid-black silhouette, no eyes, no visible face, monocle is the only feature.
3. Compositions got 'messy' / cluttered (busy space elements: planets, satellites, stars). Need cleaner, calmer compositions.
4. Text control WORKED when explicit ('absolutely no text/letters/numbers/symbols') -- keep that; add curated text only in post if wanted.

THE PLAN (when we return to images):
A. /randomize for NEW COMPOSITIONS -- don't reuse the single goose-in-biplane-in-sky comp Zig gave. Run /randomize over composition axes to force diversity, e.g.: SCENE (cockpit interior / on the tarmac / map-room war-table / rooftop stakeout / hangar / control tower / open sky), CAMERA (close two-shot / wide establishing / over-the-shoulder / low heroic / top-down), SUBJECT ARRANGEMENT (duo side-by-side / goose leading, agent following / back-to-back / conspiratorial huddle), MOOD (heist-caper / triumphant / sneaky-nefarious / cozy-partners), MEDIUM (flat vector / screenprint / gouache / cel / paper-cut), ERA-NOD (noir / 60s-spy / retrofuturist). Record the seed+provenance per /randomize discipline.
B. RESEARCH NEW AESTHETICS beyond the 8 tried -- candidates to explore: mid-century spy-thriller title-card (Saul Bass), Carmen-Sandiego cel-animation, noir woodcut, cut-paper/collage, gouache travel-poster, ligne-claire (Tintin), screenprint gig-poster, matte editorial vector. Pull Zig's cfp painterly refs (~/cfp/talk-ai-council/refs/msb-aesthetic) + the zig-voice mood-board + AAIF brand as image-to-image anchors.
C. KEEP: the character+goose DUO concept, the caper/vigilante energy, AAIF black+orange DOMINANT with legible support palette, faceless-black-mascot fidelity, strict text control.

Current best-legible asset: submissions/.../images/char2/a1-mascot.png (on metis ~/Downloads). Not final.
notes: CORRECTION to mascot fidelity (Zig 2026-07-04): the AAIF ambassador is NOT a faceless black silhouette (my earlier note was WRONG). It's a friendly cartoon secret-agent: a LIGHT/WHITE face with black cartoon line-features -- two eyes (one round with a highlight, one behind the round ORANGE dotted monocle-lens) and a big open smile -- under a black fedora, wearing a black trench coat with light lapels + buttons, an antenna with a ball tip on the hat, carrying an 'AAIF' briefcase, one white-gloved hand raised. KEY RULE: keep the FACE LIGHT with black features. Do NOT fill the character solid black -- that's the error that looked wrong. Formula: black outlines + white face + black features + orange monocle + black hat/coat. Ref: refs/brand/aaif-ambassador-white.png (clearest read of the line-art).

title: radar: 2026-W27 AAIF landscape learnings (baseline)
description:
BASELINE (first /aaif-radar run, 2026-07-03). 47 approved scorecards to date; issues to #117.

TYPE MIX (over-rotation): blog_post ~53% dominates the field — the lowest-leverage written type (15 pts). tutorial ~19%. The high-value types are near-empty: conference_talk and short_technical_video 1 each; workshop/course 0 ever.

PROJECT MIX: MCP ~66% (mostly pre-7-28 explainers); agentgateway is the thinnest of the four core projects (~17%).

UNDER-SERVED LANES (our opportunity, framed by developer value):
- tutorial x agentgateway — a high-value type on the thinnest project (the gist walkthrough, 18o.28).
- conference_talk — the single highest-value type (30), almost absent in the field.
- deep empirical/methodological content — the field is explainers; verified-against-real-data is open.
- goose local-inference + MCP 7-28 stateless — explicitly campaign-wanted, under-covered.

GRADING CALIBRATION: the documented detected_type->base_points model HOLDS with ZERO drift (tutorial 20, blog 15, talk 30, social 5, video 15, meetup 25, project_contribution 5/15/25). The only total!=base adjustment across 47 remains the known weak-project-tie meetup dock. /aaif-review predictions stay accurate — no model update needed.

DELTA 2026-07-04 (same-week re-run): +3 submissions (#118-120), 0 new scorecards (still 47) -> grading model still holds, zero drift. Two trend signals: (1) AGENTS.md work is maturing from explainers toward TOOLING + hands-on build-alongs (a linter/scaffolder and a build-along landed) — the explainer window there is closing; marginal value is now tooling + verifiable hands-on. (2) agentgateway GOVERNANCE content is beginning to appear (a virtual-keys / per-user-token-budget piece) — the thinnest core project starting to fill on the governance side. Opportunity read: the agentgateway-governance lane is warming, not empty (timely for the honk tutorial 18o.28, still differentiated as a full o11y/governance-plane TUTORIAL vs a single-feature blog); AGENTS.md's tooling shift favors the tooling-shaped ideas (18o.7 pattern catalog, 18o.13 converter) over another explainer.

title: upskill for AAIF: ship azigler/skills with re-runnable 'upskill eval' receipts (extends aaif-49y)
description:
From upskill/FINDINGS.md opportunity 2. aaif-49y (AAIF monthly-drop pipeline; first drop = azigler/skills) names the differentiator as 'attach /ab eval RECEIPTS — the thing no skills publisher ships.' But /ab structurally CANNOT be a public receipt — it runs on Zig's Claude SUBSCRIPTION via subagent dispatch, so a stranger cloning azigler/skills can't reproduce it. upskill CAN: Apache-2.0, 'pip install upskill', vendor-neutral (~699 stars) — a third party runs 'upskill eval skills/<name>' and watches the same skill-lift + token-savings land. So upskill EXTENDS /ab into the PUBLIC lane (not duplicate): /ab stays the private taste-gated internal verifier; upskill is the citable, outsider-reproducible proof the drop ships WITH the skills. Move: first azigler/skills drop ships per skill an evals/ dir + a committed 'upskill eval' results card (lift + token-savings + the test cases + provenance); README convention = 'the only agent-skill repo where every skill ships a reproducible pass-rate — re-run it yourself with upskill eval.' Pick 2-3 MECHANICALLY-checkable skills first (/commit gitmoji+trailer, proof-token wording) — never editorial ones (/zig-voice). Clears the no-slop gate by construction (a novel primitive + a real re-runnable measurement). RISK: upskill's default tests are SYNTHETIC — publishing '100% pass' on a self-written test is teaching-to-your-own-exam, WORSE than no receipt; the published receipt MUST run on a real/curated corpus and DISCLOSE provenance; never ship a synthetic-only green. Active arc: AAIF. Source: upskill/FINDINGS.md

title: spec: 'Earned Autonomy' whitepaper — section plan + verified claim inventory + grep-able test cases (aaif-51g acceptance contract)
description:
SPEC for the "Earned Autonomy" MCP governance whitepaper (parent submission aaif-51g). This is the P0 acceptance contract: the section plan, the verified claim inventory, and the grep-able test cases every drafted section must turn green. Built under the /research-paper loop. Grounding: submissions/2026-mcp-governance-whitepaper/research/p0-verified-findings.md (all facts primary-source-verified, bead aaif-wzz) + refs/projects/mcp.md (SEP source of record).

=== OVERVIEW / THESIS (framing LOCKED, Zig 2026-07-16) ===
Title (hook): "Earned Autonomy: Governing Agent Fleets on the 2026-07-28 MCP Spec." KEEP the phrase as the accessible entry point, but the SPINE leads with the genuinely-novel contribution (the phrase itself is non-distinctive; see novelty finding). Contribution = the JOIN + SUBSTRATE SWAP: autonomy earned on DELIVERED SOFTWARE OUTCOMES (change-failure/revert/held-in-production of agent-authored change) — NOT agent task-accuracy — enforced at the PER-CALL boundary the spec provides, organized by the standard's OWN governance-by-measurement (conformance gates, tiered SDKs with relegation, the lifecycle clock). Celebrate the spec as the enabler throughout; never fault it.
The delivery-outcome layer (WORKING NAME; final name TBD via /randomize at draft, dodging the context-engineering 'context layer' collision + branded labels SEI/'delivery intelligence'/'engineering intelligence') = one store, two faces: WRITE path Attribute+Evaluate; READ path Serve; plus Steer (forward agenda). Define it sharply on first use and CONTRAST it with context-engineering's opposite-meaning 'context layer' (input-to-agents vs our outcome-to-authorizer).

AUDIENCE: engineering leaders (Dir Platform/DevEx/AI-Enablement, VP Eng, CTO) in regulated/enterprise orgs. PROMISE: a leader leaves with a framework for granting/adjusting agent autonomy on evidence, an evaluation checklist, and an honest map of what the spec does vs what they must build.
LENGTH: ~5-8 pages (~4000-5000 words) + references. Practitioner-grounded citations (industry sources OK; relaxed on foundational-text formalism) — but EVERY citation real, verified, justified.

=== SECTION PLAN (6 sections; per-section word budget; what each PROVES) ===
0. Exec summary/abstract (~200w) — the thesis in one breath: the spec makes agent activity legible+enforceable; a delivery-outcome layer turns events into answers+steering; earned autonomy is the loop above the protocol.
1. The question that predates the spec (~600w) — ROI on autonomous work was ALWAYS the question; the spec doesn't create it. Stakes (VERIFIED phrasings only): NANDA ~95% of enterprise GenAI initiatives show NO MEASURABLE P&L RETURN (NOT 'reach production'); DORA-2025 AI as AMPLIFIER (positive throughput / negative stability, conditional on capability); Veracode 45% of code SAMPLES ship an OWASP Top-10; Stanford adoption 78%->88% + agents fail ~1 in 3. PROVES: the governance gap predates+outlives the protocol.
2. What the spec makes legible + enforceable (~800w) — the SEP walk, celebration EARNED: identity-bound (2575 clientInfo in _meta, 2468 unspoofable OAuth), typed (2106), traceable (414), enforceable (2567 per-call re-auth, 2243 header routing), fleet-scalable (2549 cache scopes, REQUIRED). PROVES: after 7-28 every action is a well-formed record AND a policy decision point.
3. The loop above the protocol (~500w) — atoms don't compose into answers; the protocol's horizon ends at the tool boundary BY DESIGN (no MCP call runs at merge/deploy/incident); the questions leaders ask live at the delivered change. (Relocated statelessness anchor: the gap was always there; 2567 proves only the ENABLING half. Do NOT anchor the gap to statelessness as if it created it.) PROVES: a layer above the protocol is required.
4. The delivery-outcome layer, defined (~900w) — the four capabilities: ATTRIBUTE (authorship-segment delivered change; join spec action-records to VCS/CI-CD — the join is the implementer's build) . EVALUATE (outcome signals per agent change + cost-to-outcome) . SERVE (deterministic typed freshness-contracted context back over the 7-28 substrate: 2106/2549/2567/414) . STEER (evaluations->boundary policy; forward agenda). Contrast the name w/ context-engineering. PROVES: the fuzzy 'higher layer' is concrete + non-fuzzy.
5. Earned autonomy (~900w) — the SPINE. The standard governs itself by measurement (SEP-2484 conformance gate on Accepted->Final; SDK Tier1=100%/Tier2=80% w/ 4-wk relegation; 12-mo lifecycle from revision release). Port to fleets: autonomy earned by delivered outcomes, adjusted at the per-call enforcement point. Spec=actuator+legibility; layer=sensor+evidence; policy engine between=the org's build (flag honestly). BOUNDARY CONDITIONS first-class: attribution lag (rolling windows) . unstable identity (unit of trust = a WORKFLOW CONFIG, not an instance) . Goodhart (gate on held-in-prod, never activity counts) . small-n (sandbox defaults). TWO HONESTY VALVES: (1) per-call authz = ENFORCEMENT POINT, scope semantics stay the implementer's (authN != authZ-scope); (2) per-call identity = principal + client, NOT individual-agent (agent attribution is a convention you build). Distinguish from existing 'earned/progressive autonomy' (all condition on task-accuracy; we condition on delivered outcomes + enforce per-call). PROVES: the framework, credible under its own failure modes.
6. Recommendations + evaluation checklist (~600w) — what a leader does Monday; budget the layer in the same cycle as the MCP boundary; the checklist (attribute at change-level, close governed-action->shipped-result, segment authorship, resist adoption proxies, serve context over governed rails). PROVES: actionable.

=== CLAIM INVENTORY (every load-bearing claim -> verified source) ===
All facts in research/p0-verified-findings.md (primary-source-verified). Load-bearing:
- SEP facts (2567/2575/2468/2243/414/2106/2549) -> refs/projects/mcp.md + draft changelog.
- Govern-by-measurement (2484 gate on Accepted->Final; SDK tiers+relegation; 2596/2577 lifecycle) -> sdk-tiers + feature-lifecycle pages + PR#2484.

- Stats: NANDA no-P&L-return / DORA amplifier / Veracode 45% of samples / Stanford 78%-88% + 1-in-3 -> primary URLs in p0-verified-findings.md.

=== OUT OF SCOPE (won't do) ===
- No vendor/product/proof point on any page (vendor-neutral hard gate). No LinearB, no adopting a branded category label.
- No new empirical study (positioning/framework paper; the reference-implementation evidence in data/ is illustrative, not a controlled experiment).
- No stateless-core migration how-to (saturated lane; we walk the SEPs for what they make MEASURABLE, not how to migrate).
- No academic-layer machinery (Zenodo/arXiv/ORCID) unless a peer-reviewed venue enters scope.

=== TEST CASES (grep-able; ~14; the P2/P3 acceptance gates) — format TEST/INPUT/EXPECTED ===

notes:
FABLE REVIEW v2 — FOLD INTO DRAFTING (2026-07-16; SUPERSEDES the section notes in the description where they conflict). All adopted; Zig-confirm items flagged.

LEAD = THE INDICTMENT, not differentiation. The substrate-swap as originally landed reads defensive ('theirs grades accuracy, ours grades outcomes'). Weaponize it: task-accuracy conditioning RE-CREATES the clean-record trap at the TRUST layer. A 99%-tool-call-correct agent still ships the Veracode defect; an accuracy-graded autonomy ladder promotes a workflow at the exact moment its output is least examined. Behavior-graded trust inherits every known failure of activity metrics; OUTCOME-graded trust is the only version that closes the loop. Turn 'you've heard this' into an asset: 'the version you've heard grades the wrong variable.' The swap MUST be visible AT FIRST CONTACT (title/exec-summary), because the pattern-match to progressive-autonomy slideware happens at the title, before Section 5 can run the distinction.

SUBTITLE (working, [ZIG CONFIRM]): "Earned Autonomy: Conditioning Agent Trust on Delivered Outcomes, Not Task Accuracy." (X-not-Y house syntax; echoes 'measure leverage, not adoption'.) If not adopted, the exec-summary FIRST SENTENCE carries the swap.

CITE PRIOR ART early + by name (Roder 'Earned Autonomy Gradient'; 'progressive autonomy'; the Digital Apprentice arXiv paper) in Section 1 or at the top of the indictment. Citing+turning reads authoritative; ignoring reads unread/appropriative.

CENTRAL ATTACKABLE CLAIM (stake ONE; do NOT tour the three legs) — draft: "Autonomy conditioned on task accuracy re-creates at the trust layer the same clean-record trap the audit layer already has; delivered outcome is the only conditioning variable that closes the loop, and the 2026-07-28 spec is the first standard substrate on which that condition is enforceable per action." A skeptic can attack that sentence = that is what makes it a contribution; a tour of legs cannot be attacked = retread.

ILLUSTRATIVE EXHIBIT (RESOLVES OQ4 = YES, include it): ONE half-page sidebar — a ~6-line gateway policy conditioning a scope grant on a workflow's trailing revert rate. Labeled ILLUSTRATIVE; NO product noun. Cheapest answer to the slideware read; shows Steer-as-cadence is CONFIG not research; plays the builder credential (the differentiated asset). Resist any wedge beyond the one claim + this one exhibit (defensive accretion turns 'synthesis' into an apology; a sharp claim makes it a compliment).

STEER = THE LOAD-BEARING LEG (not a hedged 'forward agenda' item). Without Steer the paper is Attribute+Evaluate (SEI's territory) + Serve (context-engineering's) = a synthesis of two existing categories. Steer turns the join into a LOOP and is WHERE MCP EARNS ITS PLACE (the actuator: per-call authz + gateway policy on operation headers). Demote it and the celebrate-MCP polarity collapses to 'the spec gives you attribution markers.' DEFINE STEER AS A CADENCE, NOT A CONTROLLER — precedent is the SAME thing the paper ports: MCP's steer leg is humans publishing SDK tiers on measured conformance, with defined clocks + a relegation rule; nobody built an automated controller and the governance works. Earned autonomy matches: a ROLLING TRUST REVIEW in which workflow configurations hold published tiers, delivery-evidence-layer signals are the input, promotion/relegation follow defined triggers, and the resulting policy is enforced per-call at the boundary. Every piece is mechanically expressible TODAY; the only novel input is swapping static scope grants for evidence-conditioned ones. AUTOMATING the cadence is the forward agenda (stated as such). Under this framing the 4 boundary conditions become DESIGN PARAMETERS of the cadence — window length / trust unit (a workflow config) / gating signals (held-in-prod) / cold-start defaults (sandbox) — not caveats on a speculative controller.

SECTION 3 RESCOPE — from 'the loop above the protocol' (a 500w corridor built on the spatial metaphor the relocated anchor no longer needs; it collapses into Section 2) TO the TRAP-AND-INDICTMENT section. New job: the limit of the record (the clean-audit trap — a fully authorized/traced/schema-valid call opens a defective PR while the record reads clean; Veracode carries the stakes here) + task-accuracy conditioning as the SAME limit one level up. HEADER: "What the record cannot tell you." (Avoids celebration+indictment whiplash inside Section 2; sets up Section 4's layer as the supplier of the missing variable; lets Section 5 run the framework without pausing to differentiate mid-argument.)

SECTION 4 opens with the ATTRIBUTION GAP (named + cited: DORA cannot distinguish AI-authored from human-authored change). Converts ATTRIBUTE from abstract-capability-list-item-1 into the FIX for a defect the reader's existing metrics program is already known to have; anchors the layer to programs leaders already run (DORA, DX Core 4) rather than to a new free-floating category.

SECTION 6 = carry the operating CADENCE from Steer as the Monday move (published workflow tiers, review rhythm, relegation triggers, cold-start defaults). Institutable WITHOUT buying anything = what makes a vendor-neutral paper actionable. The current checklist reads acquisition-flavored; replace it with the cadence.

SECTION 2 (800w) holds IF the SEP walk REFERENCES mechanics rather than re-explaining them.

LAYER NAME leading candidate: "the delivery evidence layer" (name by the novel FUNCTION — evidence feeds the AUTHORIZER; context feeds the ACTOR; 'evidence' is the word earned autonomy runs on, so framework + layer share a vocabulary). 'delivery-outcome layer' weaker ('outcome' = what it holds, not what it's for). /RANDOMIZE GATE at draft: the name must survive being SAID ALOUD ON A PODCAST with no definition attached (that is the distribution channel — a name needing a paragraph dies in a news segment). Final pick still at draft per Zig; delivery-evidence-layer is the leading candidate.

FOUR RIPPLES (uncaught in P0):
(a) NANDA = EXHIBIT A, not generic doom. 'No measurable P&L return' is measurement-shaped: a return you cannot ATTRIBUTE is a return you cannot CLAIM. Use it in Section 1 as the thesis's motivating SYMPTOM (at minimum an attribution failure). GUARD CAUSALITY: the 95% has many causes — write 'at minimum an attribution failure', NEVER 'because'.
(b) DORA conditional = EVIDENCE FOR THE FRAMEWORK, not only a stake. The amplifier being conditional on team capability is the empirical echo of the argument: the organizational property that determines whether AI speed helps or hurts IS the feedback capability the paper tells you to build. Put in Section 5 or 6 as CORROBORATION; do not bury it in the Section 1 stakes.
(c) Stanford agents-fail-~1-in-3 = a FRAMING TRAP. It is a TASK-ACCURACY stat; deployed as an opening stake it re-anchors the reader in the exact frame the paper indicts. Use it INSIDE the indictment (accuracy is weak AND it is the wrong variable) or DROP it. Adoption figures (78->88) are safe as stakes.
(d) CNCF caveat: graduation criteria are partly QUALITATIVE (adopters, audits, governance maturity). Call it 'evidence-based maturation WITH HUMAN RATIFICATION' (conveniently matches Steer-as-cadence); NEVER 'purely metric-gated'.

T19 steer-is-cadence: Section 5 frames Steer as a rolling review/cadence w/ tiers+triggers, and labels automation as forward agenda (manual + grep 'cadence'|'rolling').

Keep ONE crisp category noun in the paper. 3-month arc syntactic continuity: July 'measure leverage not adoption' -> paper 'trust outcomes not accuracy' -> Sept 'here is the ROI'. title: idea: 'Two gateways, two problems' — LiteLLM vs agentgateway explainer + decision guide
description:
ANGLE: a clarifying explainer answering the question every dev evaluating agentgateway asks — "how is this different from LiteLLM, which has ~10x the GitHub stars?" Frame = "two gateways, two problems," NOT "agentgateway beats LiteLLM." Genuinely fair to LiteLLM (best-in-class LLM proxy). Origin: Zig hit this exact question himself 2026-07-11.

THESIS: LiteLLM and agentgateway overlap on the LLM-proxy square but are shaped for different-sized problems from opposite starting points. LiteLLM = "call any of 100+ models through one OpenAI-compatible API + track the bill" (LLM-first; Python SDK + proxy; ~53k stars; the problem nearly everyone has today, and older). agentgateway = "govern + observe a whole agent fleet's traffic — LLM + MCP + A2A — as one network data plane" (protocol-plane-first; purpose-built Rust; ~3.5k stars; the problem that's arriving). The star gap measures problem-ubiquity + age, not quality — say so plainly.

DIFFERENTIATION AXES (the body):
1. CENTER OF GRAVITY — LLM-first vs protocol-plane. LiteLLM added MCP/A2A as endpoints; its MCP is LLM-endpoint-centric (register servers, call tools THROUGH /chat/completions). agentgateway treats LLM/MCP/A2A as peers over one data plane; virtual-MCP federation (multiplex many MCP servers into one endpoint, tool-name prefixing, per-tool CEL authz, unauthorized tools filtered out of `list` entirely = tool-poisoning defense) is the deepest MCP support in the space. "LiteLLM is an LLM gateway that learned some MCP; agentgateway is an agent-traffic gateway where MCP is first-class."
2. ARCHITECTURE — Python library-that-grew-a-proxy vs purpose-built Rust data plane. Load-bearing rationale: MCP's stateful, bidirectional streaming mismatched the stateless model of existing proxies (Envoy) -> they built a new one; hence Gateway-API-conformant, mTLS, xDS hot-reload (real network infra, not an app-tier service). PERF: vendor benchmarks ~11.5x throughput / ~10x lower latency / 22MB vs 11.8GB memory. MUST cite as VENDOR-published (Solo.io / Lin Sun) AND link the reproducible repo (github.com/linsun/litellm-agw-perf) so readers can re-run; never launder as neutral fact.
3. GOVERNANCE — both do virtual keys / budgets / spend / guardrails (LiteLLM excellent at multi-tenant cost). agentgateway differentiator = uniform CEL authorization across ALL three protocols + the "kill-switch" posture (nothing reaches a model/tool except through the gateway; per-agent token budget + tool allowlist; one JWT revocation halts a rogue agent). LiteLLM governs spend + model access; agentgateway governs capability + blast radius.

DECISION GUIDE (a table): when LiteLLM (unify providers, Python app, multi-tenant spend, most mature here) vs when agentgateway (MCP tool governance, network-grade authz/mTLS/Gateway-API, fleet-wide enforcement/kill-switch, proxy-tier perf). Non-obvious closer: it's NOT strictly either/or — you can run LiteLLM as a provider behind agentgateway, or use each for the layer it's best at. If you only need the LLM-proxy third, LiteLLM is the pragmatic pick today.

HOOK / SPINE: convergence — both moving toward "the agentic gateway" from opposite ends (LiteLLM up from the LLM-proxy side adding MCP/A2A; agentgateway out from the network-data-plane side including LLM). "Where a tool starts reveals what it optimizes for." This is the concrete, accessible on-ramp to the aaif-715 "harness/loop/gateway are converging" perspective piece — could pair or feed it.

ANTI-SLOP GATES (before any publish):
- VENDOR-NEUTRAL: fair to LiteLLM (it's genuinely excellent at its job); "two problems," never "X beats Y"; no benchmark-war framing (they already published the benchmarks — echoing them reads as vendor slop).
- VERIFY (both move fast): benchmarks are vendor -> link repo, don't launder. Re-characterize LiteLLM's MCP/A2A support against CURRENT docs (ideally a hands-on check) before publish — the "MCP through /chat/completions" claim needs a fresh confirm. Confirm star / provider counts at publish time.
- REAL USAGE: Zig speaks first-person on the agentgateway side (real deploy on pico, the /agentgateway skill, the shipped yw5 tutorial). Don't assert LiteLLM first-person unless he actually runs it.

RADAR FIT (W28 scan): agentgateway explainer content is the MOST under-served lane while intro-MCP blogs are saturated -> high developer pull, low competition.

title: agentgateway upstream: config-driven custom UI dimensions (map a header/attribute -> filterable chip) + attested-identity binding
description:
CONSIDER-LATER (Zig 2026-07-13, 'i dont know if we need it'). Source: the durable-session-identity work in ~/explore (explore-qmsy) + a 2-researcher comparison (agentgateway vs LiteLLM/Portkey/Helicone).

THE GAP (verified, no prior art in agentgateway issues/PRs): agentgateway has NO config-driven way to promote a custom header/attribute to a UI filter dimension/chip. Peers all ship this:
- LiteLLM: extra_spend_tag_headers: [x-agent-name] -> header captured as a filterable tag.
- Portkey: HEADERS_TO_METADATA -> header -> filterable/group-by metadata.
- Helicone: Helicone-Property-<Name> -> auto filterable custom property.
agentgateway's /ui hardcodes exactly 5 dimensions (model/user/group/provider/userAgent) in the SPA (array A7 + validator) AND the backend returns a FIXED filterOptions set. BUT the backend query layer already group/filters by ARBITRARY attributes_json keys (proven live via POST /api/logs/search + /api/logs/analytics/summary with {field:'attributes',key:'session'|'agent'}). So it's mostly plumbing: un-hardcode the SPA dimension list (render from filterOptions/config) + include configured custom keys in filterOptions.

PROPOSAL SHAPE: a config knob e.g. standardAttributes.custom: [{key,label}] or telemetry.ui.dimensions: [...] that (1) adds those keys to filterOptions and (2) has the SPA render dimensions from config/filterOptions instead of the hardcoded array.

THE DIFFERENTIATOR (why it's a DISTINCTIVE agentgateway contribution, not just catch-up): every peer's label is SELF-ASSERTED + spoofable (LiteLLM even has a client-tags-overwrite-admin-tags bug, #27134). agentgateway sits at extAuthz->tailscaled with an ATTESTED tailnet caller identity, so it can BIND the custom dimension to a VERIFIED caller. That's the 'attribution -> enforcement' upgrade (the crypto-later bridge from explore-qmsy). No pure-observability gateway can match it.

STATUS: interim need already met locally WITHOUT this (mapped our durable identity onto the native 'user' dimension via standardAttributes.user CEL -> existing 'User' chip, zero fork). So this upstream feature is a NICE-TO-HAVE for an arbitrarily-LABELED chip ('agent'/'session') + the attested-binding angle, NOT a blocker. Decide later whether to file an agentgateway issue/PR.

title: spec: rewrite 'If you give a Goose an MCP server' — substance-first re-architecture + skills 5->1 + workflow hardening
description:
GOAL: Re-architect the AAIF tutorial "If you give a Goose an MCP server" into a genuinely useful, substantive, technical-leader-respecting agentgateway tutorial; consolidate the 5 gateway-* skills into ONE agentgateway skill FIRST; then harden the draft via a subagent workflow; then ship to the review gist (after Zig's okay), gist FIRST then metis. Zig-directed after he rejected the v5 "nursery-rhyme" draft as over-whimsical, vaporous, and structurally gapped. Execute end-to-end in a FRESH session (this one is context-saturated); onboard reads this bead + .local/goose-tutorial-execution.md + the handoff note.

WHY (v5 gaps to fix):
1. NO model-setup step. The tutorial routes the local model through the gateway (the whole o11y point) yet never configures it. The real first step is configuring the model.
2. The MCP step never explains the actual move. It must teach: federate raw MCP servers as targets -> the gateway VIRTUALIZES them into ONE endpoint -> scope that endpoint per-key with CEL so each agent/session discovers only its allowed tools. That per-agent scoping is the core value and is currently absent.
3. Too much AI/whimsy language ("Here's the half most local setups skip"...); vaporous; not technical enough; would not earn a technical leader's respect. Dial whimsy WAY back.
4. Skills section buries real work. 5 is bloat; "watch" is misleading (a skill can't watch continuously); no examples of what each is or how they combine. -> ONE agentgateway skill with components + real usage examples.
5. Step 5 "parts that will trip you" -> CUT entirely.
6. "Why a gateway and not just a smaller server" -> false comparison (a gateway IS a server); bad header; useless copy -> CUT.
7. The "If You Give a Mouse a Cookie" conceit is MISUSED in places. Recognizable children's story; use it correctly. The FORMAT is good -> use it for the SECTION HEADERS / structure (the "zoom out" moves), not scattered cutesy lines.

SUBSTANCE TO TEACH (verified vs the live config + agentgateway docs; generic shapes here, private IPs/paths in .local):
- MODEL SETUP: agentgateway `llm` block = a `provider` (e.g. `provider: ollama`, `params.baseUrl: http://localhost:11434/v1`) + a `models:` list, each `provider: {reference: <name>}`. Register your local model -> model calls route through the gateway's LLM listener, so model calls AND tool calls land in the SAME request log. Be opinionated: "I'm using Ollama + qwen3:8b; use whatever you like." Fold the context-window reality here: small models default to a tight window (~4096 for qwen3:8b); raise with `num_ctx` / env `OLLAMA_CONTEXT_LENGTH` (~32768) or tool schemas overrun the context and the model silently stops calling tools.
- MCP VIRTUALIZATION + SCOPING (the spine): add each raw MCP server as a `target` (name + stdio cmd) under the `mcp` block = the federated pile. The gateway presents them as ONE virtual MCP endpoint (multiplexing). `mcpAuthorization` CEL rules scope per key: `apiKey.name == "goose" && mcp.tool.name in [read_text_file, write_file, edit_file, list_directory, search_files, fetch]` grants exactly six; a broader key (cc) sees all. Unauthorized tools are FILTERED from tools/list (the client never discovers them). Give each agent/session its own key -> per-agent/per-session tool scoping off one virtual server. tiny-tools = the goose key's 6-tool view. Docs: https://agentgateway.dev/docs/standalone/latest/tutorials/mcp-federation/ | https://agentgateway.dev/docs/standalone/main/mcp/mcp-authz/ | https://agentgateway.dev/docs/kubernetes/latest/mcp/virtual/
- FACTS TO PRESERVE (fact-checked, do not re-break): filesystem server = 14 tools, NO delete tool; write_file overwrites (the destructive one, KEPT because the task writes files); move_file "fails if destination exists" (does NOT overwrite; CUT for task-need not danger); fetch = 1 tool; federated = 15; keep 6; move_file probe -> status=400 "Unknown tool: filesystem_move_file"; access-log (tool calls) vs request-log DB (model calls) split; agentgateway self-describes as "connectivity solution / proxy," NOT "data plane" (don't overclaim). Full list + config shapes: .local/goose-tutorial-execution.md.

RE-ARCHITECTURE (substance-first; longer is fine; doc links where they help; zero cutesy filler; title kept as the hook; nursery-rhyme = correct light touch in the SECTION HEADERS/structure only):
  1. Put your model behind the gateway (provider + model; opinionated Ollama+qwen3:8b; num_ctx/context window here). NEW; needs a Models-view screenshot (redact the ollama IP -> localhost).
  2. Federate your MCP servers into one virtual endpoint (targets -> one endpoint).
  3. Scope the endpoint per agent (per-key CEL; unauthorized tools vanish; the CORE value; tiny-tools = goose's cut).
  4. Watch it all in one place (model + tool calls in one log — the payoff of routing both).
  5. Prove the scope holds (move_file -> "Unknown tool" probe).
  6. One agentgateway skill (audit/cost/trace/harden as components + the request-log cookbook reference; real examples of running them as a loop; DROP "watch").
  + Team-scale closer (per-agent scoping -> role-scoped keys). CUT the gotchas step + the "why a gateway vs a server" section.

PHASE 0 — SKILLS CONSOLIDATION (do FIRST): merge aaif/.claude/skills/gateway-{audit,cost,watch,trace,harden} into ONE `agentgateway` skill. Keep audit/cost/trace/harden as documented components; DROP "watch"; the request-log cookbook (refs/gateway-request-log-cookbook.md) is the shared reference. Include real loop examples (audit the morning after -> trace a surprising call -> cost a heavy run -> harden proposes a tighter allowlist for review; read-only, human-gated, harden never applies). Update CLAUDE.md routing + file layout to one skill; update the tutorial's skill section to link it.

PHASE 1 — REWRITE (lead, grounded): produce the restructured, de-whimsied, substantive draft. Capture the new Model-setup screenshot(s) from the live gateway UI (rig + redaction in .local). Reuse existing UI screenshots (servers, tools, tool-call) where they fit.

PHASE 2 — WORKFLOW HARDENING (use the Workflow tool; Zig-directed): parallel fresh-eyes critiques on the Phase-1 draft, each a distinct lens: (a) technical-leader-respect (substantive + accurate? would a staff/principal engineer respect it?); (b) GPT-ism/AI-language audit (list every tell; zig-voice banned list); (c) helpfulness (can a reader DO this end to end? what's under-explained/missing?); (d) voice x2 — one pass WITH /zig-voice, one WITHOUT; (e) STORY FIDELITY — a dedicated agent reading the piece against "If You Give a Mouse a Cookie" to verify the conceit is used CORRECTLY (not misused) and to propose using the format for the section headers/structure. Then a SYNTHESIS agent merges into a hardened rewrite; then a FINAL COLD READ (fresh agent, no prior context). Verify EVERY technical claim survives; no GPT-isms; story correct.

DELIVERY: land in the gist once ready. Order: Zig reviews -> okay -> push to the GIST FIRST -> THEN the metis Obsidian vault (blog). Paths/frontmatter in .local. Gist must render all images.

ACCEPTANCE:
[ ] Model-setup section: present, accurate, redacted Models-view screenshot.
[ ] MCP virtualization + per-agent (per-key CEL) scoping = the clearly-explained core value.
[ ] Whimsy = correct light nursery-rhyme touch in headers/structure; body substantive + technical; opinionated stack; doc links where they help; zero cutesy filler.
[ ] 5 gateway-* skills -> ONE agentgateway skill (components + examples; no "watch"); CLAUDE.md + tutorial updated.
[ ] gotchas step + "why a gateway vs a server" CUT.
[ ] Story-fidelity verified (mouse-cookie used correctly; format in section headers).
[ ] Workflow hardening run (parallel critiques -> synthesis -> cold read); all facts verified; no GPT-isms.
[ ] After Zig's okay: pushed to the gist first, then metis; gist renders all images.
